title: penstemon newberryi var. berryi
intro: Penstemon newberryi var. berryi, also known as Berry's beardtongue, is a variety of the Penstemon newberryi plant, a type of flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and sore throats, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Penstemon newberryi var. berryi can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
